test_cases.title: Viewer can successfully view project details
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task, one comment, and populated project details
A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user
Send a GET request (or navigate) to the project details endpoint for the project
Inspect the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns HTTP 200 with the full project details visible to the viewer

test_cases.title: Viewer can successfully view tasks and comments
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task containing at least one comment
A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user
Send a GET request to the tasks list endpoint for the project
Verify task list is returned
Send a GET request to the comments endpoint for one of the tasks
Inspect the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: Both requests return HTTP 200 with the full list of tasks and comments respectively

test_cases.title: Viewer is denied when attempting to create a task
preconditions: A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user
Send a POST request to the task creation endpoint for the project with a valid task payload
Inspect the response status and error message
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns an access-denied error (e.g., HTTP 403) and the task is not created in the project

test_cases.title: Viewer is denied when attempting to edit or delete a task
preconditions: A project exists with at least one existing task
A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user
Send a PUT or PATCH request to the task update endpoint with a valid edit payload targeting the existing task
Inspect the response status and error message
Send a DELETE request to the task deletion endpoint targeting the same existing task
Inspect the response status and error message
test_cases.expected_result: Both the edit and delete requests return an access-denied error (e.g., HTTP 403) and the task remains unchanged and present in the project

test_cases.title: Viewer is denied when attempting to add a comment
preconditions: A project exists with at least one existing task
A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user
Send a POST request to the comment creation endpoint for an existing task with a valid comment payload
Inspect the response status and error message
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns an access-denied error (e.g., HTTP 403) and no new comment is persisted on the task

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated or unauthorized actor cannot bypass viewer write restrictions by manipulating the role claim
preconditions: A project exists with at least one existing task
A user account exists with the 'viewer' role assigned to the project
steps: Authenticate as the viewer-role user and obtain a valid session token or JWT
Manually modify the role claim in the token to a higher-privilege role (e.g., 'editor' or 'admin') without a valid signature
Send a POST request to the task creation endpoint using the tampered token with a valid task payload
Inspect the response status and error message
test_cases.expected_result: The server rejects the tampered token and returns an access-denied or authentication error (e.g., HTTP 401 or 403); no task is created

assumptions: The system enforces role-based access control server-side, not solely on the client side
An 'access-denied error' maps to HTTP 403 Forbidden; HTTP 401 Unauthorized is assumed to be distinct (unauthenticated) unless the system conflates them
Read operations (view) are implemented as HTTP GET requests and write operations use POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE
The viewer role is scoped per-project; a user may have different roles in different projects
Comments are associated with tasks within a project, not standalone project-level entities
notes: The requirement does not specify the error response format (e.g., JSON error body structure, error code, or human-readable message) beyond 'access-denied error' — test cases validate HTTP status only and the absence of side effects. It is ambiguous whether 'view comments' means comments on tasks only or also project-level comments; this is assumed to be task-level. No mention is made of editing or deleting comments — it is unclear whether a viewer is also restricted from these write actions on comments; this is a coverage gap. The requirement does not address what happens when a viewer accesses a project they are not a member of at all (no role) — this is not covered by these test cases.
